name: Arrow Transportation SVC LLC
districts_or_customers: Dayton OH metro special needs/wheelchair transport
recent_news: Listed in Dayton-area Yellow Pages as a special-needs/wheelchair-accessible transportation provider at 3164 Salem Ave (~12 years in business per directory listing). No M&A news identified.
fit_notes: Very small (13 employees, ~$0.9M revenue) likely NEMT/wheelchair operator with limited evidence of K-12 yellow-bus contracts. Too sub-scale for a Summit tuck-in unless bundled; primarily disability/medical transport rather than home-to-school routing.

name: S S Transportation
districts_or_customers: Matawan-Aberdeen / Monmouth County NJ school districts (special-needs student routes)
recent_news: Locally owned/operated since 2008; actively recruiting school bus drivers in 2025-2026. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Independent family-owned NJ special-needs school transporter with ~17 years of history; very small fleet but in-state for Summit (NJ). Potential bolt-on to existing NJ density; succession unknown but multiple owner-operators suggests possible roll-up candidate.

name: Easy Transportation
recent_news: Listed on Yelp as Easy Transportation in Paramount CA (updated 2026); no detailed business profile, web presence, or M&A news identified. No confirmation of K-12 school bus contracts.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (11 employees, ~$0.8M revenue) with no confirmed yellow-bus school contracts; appears more likely to be NEMT/paratransit. Not a meaningful Summit tuck-in absent confirmation of school-district business.

name: Bec N Call Wheelchair Trnsprtn
districts_or_customers: Allen, McKinney, Plano TX metro - medical appointments, dialysis, chemo, hospital/rehab transfers
recent_news: Founded 1998 by Rebecca Calvin; sold to Kimberly Hannan (former Licensed Nursing Home Administrator) after Calvin's 2012 death/illness. FMCSA: 9 power units, 9 drivers, intrastate-only passenger carrier (USDOT 2930272).
fit_notes: NOT a school-bus operator - this is a NEMT/wheelchair/personal-services business with no K-12 home-to-school contracts identified. Out of scope for Summit's student-transportation tuck-in strategy.

name: Bay Area Wheelchair Transport
recent_news: No website, social, or news identified; listed only via business directory at 1457 Pearson Ave, San Leandro. No confirmation of K-12 student transport contracts. East Bay NEMT/wheelchair niche is crowded (Valiant, Family Care, Eden, One Access, etc.).
fit_notes: Name and small size strongly suggest a NEMT/wheelchair operator, not a school-bus contractor. Out of scope for Summit's tuck-in strategy absent evidence of school-district business.
